perturbation.original_text: SIERRA hereby appoints ENVISION as its exclusive distributor for the Product in any blend with Krill Oil within the Territory subject to ENVISION fulfilling the terms and conditions of the best efforts marketing requirements set forth herein in Sections 4, 5, and 9. SIERRA shall cease making sales to any customer or distributor who, during the term of this Agreement, violates ENVISION's exclusivity.
perturbation.changed_text: SIERRA hereby appoints ENVISION as its exclusive dealer for the Product in any blend with Krill Oil within the Territory subject to ENVISION fulfilling the terms and conditions of the best efforts marketing requirements set forth herein in Sections 4, 5, and 9. SIERRA shall cease making sales to any customer or distributor who, during the term of this Agreement, violates ENVISION's exclusivity.
perturbation.explanation: The term "distributor" is replaced with "dealer". While seemingly similar, without a clear definition, the legal obligations and rights of a 'dealer' versus a 'distributor' could be interpreted differently, creating uncertainty. The contract later refers to ENVISION's exclusivity as a distributor but now the term changed to dealer, an In-Text contradiction and uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SIERRA hereby appoints ENVISION as its exclusive dealer for the Product in any blend with Krill Oil within the Territory subject to ENVISION fulfilling the terms and conditions of the best efforts marketing requirements set forth herein in Sections 4, 5, and 9. SIERRA shall cease making sales to any customer or distributor who, during the term of this Agreement, violates ENVISION's exclusivity.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'distributor' to 'dealer' introduces a potential ambiguity as explained, but the contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text. The contradiction is triggered because 'distributor' was changed to 'dealer' in the section of text that defines Envison, creating a logical contradiction with the original intent of the text when it references Envison's exclusivity, as it no longer matches the original term. However, the fact that both the changed text and contradicted text both say 'dealer' now means they are no longer in contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The price for the Product as set forth in Section 9 herein, sold by SIERRA to ENVISION, shall be subject to change due to changes in manufacturing costs and so as to maximize profits; any changes in price for the Product shall not be applicable to previously accepted orders and shall be made with at least ninety (90) days advance notice in writing and in good faith by conference of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The cost for the Product as set forth in Section 9 herein, sold by SIERRA to ENVISION, shall be subject to change due to changes in manufacturing costs and so as to maximize profits; any changes in cost for the Product shall not be applicable to previously accepted orders and shall be made with at least ninety (90) days advance notice in writing and in good faith by conference of the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The word 'price' is replaced with 'cost'. While often used synonymously, in a business context, 'price' is what the customer pays, while 'cost' is what the seller incurs. This could lead to disputes on how changes are calculated. For example, Sierra could argue that it's the cost that can change based on manufacturing costs, but Envision could argue that this impact the final 'price' of product, an In-Text contradiction and uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, ENVISION will exclusively purchase the Product from SIERRA. The parties mutually agree to the Purchase Price of: Product Purchase Price ----------------------------------------------- A. SierraSil Per Sierra Sil's wholesale price list.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'price' to 'cost' introduces a contradiction. The original text referred to 'Purchase Price' in the contradicted text. The change allows Sierra to adjust the 'cost' based on manufacturing while Envision believes they should only be paying attention to purchase 'price' and not be impacted by manufacturing costs. This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. This aligns with the definition, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: ENVISION warrants that the Finished Product will not be adulterated or misbranded within the meaning of any federal, state, or local law or regulation or other applicable law. In the event of any breach of this warranty or in the event any user of the Finished Product makes a claim that the Finished Product was the cause of personal injury or property damage (product liability claim), ENVISION shall indemnify, defend, and hold SIERRA harmless from any liability occasioned by a breach of warranty or a product liability claim. ENVISION warrants that it carries general liability insurance of $1 million per occurrence and product liability insurance of not less than $2 million per occurrence and that, upon execution of this Agreement, it will name SIERRA as an additional insured on such policies.
perturbation.changed_text: ENVISION ensures that the Finished Product will not be adulterated or misbranded within the meaning of any federal, state, or local law or regulation or other applicable law. In the event of any breach of this warranty or in the event any user of the Finished Product makes a claim that the Finished Product was the cause of personal injury or property damage (product liability claim), ENVISION shall indemnify, defend, and hold SIERRA harmless from any liability occasioned by a breach of warranty or a product liability claim. ENVISION warrants that it carries general liability insurance of $1 million per occurrence and product liability insurance of not less than $2 million per occurrence and that, upon execution of this Agreement, it will name SIERRA as an additional insured on such policies.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'warrants' to 'ensures' alters the legal weight of the statement. A 'warranty' is a specific contractual guarantee, while 'ensures' is a more general term. This introduces confusion because other statements in the section are explicitly warranties, but now the text is using "ensures" which is not a warranty, creating an In-Text contradiction. This could cause disputes over liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ENVISION ensures that the Finished Product will not be adulterated or misbranded within the meaning of any federal, state, or local law or regulation or other applicable law. In the event of any breach of this warranty or in the event any user of the Finished Product makes a claim that the Finished Product was the cause of personal injury or property damage (product liability claim), ENVISION shall indemnify, defend, and hold SIERRA harmless from any liability occasioned by a breach of warranty or a product liability claim. ENVISION warrants that it carries general liability insurance of $1 million per occurrence and product liability insurance of not less than $2 million per occurrence and that, upon execution of this Agreement, it will name SIERRA as an additional insured on such policies.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'warrants' to 'ensures' introduces an in-text contradiction. While the paragraph continues to use 'warranty' in later clauses related to indemnification, the initial shift to 'ensures' undermines the legal specificity expected of a warranty. This inconsistency creates confusion regarding the nature and enforceability of ENVISION's obligation, thus contradicting the other 'warranty' statements in the text, fitting the definition of misaligned terminology as 'uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'
